function.arguments: {"draft": {"title": "Charred Carrots with Lemon-Herb Yogurt", "concept": "smoky charred carrots with a bright, acidic lemon-herb yogurt sauce", "flavor_rationale": [{"claim": "yogurt acidity balances the char", "provenance": null, "cuisine_context": "western"}, {"claim": "lemon juice brightens the yogurt and lifts the dish's overall acidity", "provenance": null, "cuisine_context": "western"}, {"claim": "fresh herbs add aromatic lift and freshness that complement the earthy sweetness of charred carrots", "provenance": null, "cuisine_context": "western"}], "ingredients": [{"name": "carrot", "fdc_id": null, "foodon_id": null, "qty": 500, "unit": "g"}, {"name": "chicken thigh", "fdc_id": null, "foodon_id": null, "qty": 400, "unit": "g"}, {"name": "greek yogurt", "fdc_id": null, "foodon_id": null, "qty": 150, "unit": "ml"}, {"name": "lemon", "fdc_id": null, "foodon_id": null, "qty": 1, "unit": "whole"}, {"name": "fresh dill", "fdc_id": null, "foodon_id": null, "qty": 15, "unit": "g"}, {"name": "fresh mint", "fdc_id": null, "foodon_id": null, "qty": 10, "unit": "g"}], "steps": [{"text": "Roast the carrots at 220C until charred.", "technique": "roast", "internal_temp_c": null, "why": "char concentrates sweetness"}, {"text": "Grill the chicken thighs.", "technique": "grill", "internal_temp_c": 74, "why": "food safety"}, {"text": "Mix the yogurt with lemon juice, zest, chopped dill, and chopped mint. Season with salt.", "technique": "raw", "internal_temp_c": null, "why": "creates a bright, acidic, herbaceous sauce that contrasts the charred carrots and grilled chicken"}], "constraints": {"dietary": null, "allergens": null, "equipment": null, "skill": "intermediate", "servings": 2, "on_hand": null, "cuisine": "western"}, "analysis": {"cost": {"total_usd": 6.4, "per_serving_usd": 3.2, "approximate": true, "missing": null}, "nutrition": {"calories": 320, "protein_g": 21, "fat_g": 14, "sat_fat_g": 3.5, "carbs_g": 28, "fiber_g": 7, "sugar_g": 12, "sodium_mg": 640, "unverified": null}}}, "rationale": "The cook asked to lean brighter and more acidic. This move pivots the dish from a simple \"yogurt balances char\" concept toward a more vibrant, lemon-forward direction. The title and concept are revised to center lemon and herbs as the brightening agents. Lemon is added as an ingredient (whole fruit, providing both juice and zest for layered citrus character), along with fresh dill and mint — herbs that read as bright, fresh, and aromatic in Western cuisine. A new step is added instructing the cook to mix the yogurt with lemon juice, zest, and chopped herbs, transforming a plain dollop of yogurt into a composed sauce. The lemon's acidity sharply lifts the dish, the zest adds aromatic oils, and the herbs contribute green, cooling notes that play against both the charred sweetness of the carrots and the savory grill on the chicken. All three flavor-rationale claims carry null provenance and are listed in unverified, as no grounding evidence is supplied.", "citations": [], "confidence": 0.85, "unverified": ["yogurt acidity balances the char", "lemon juice brightens the yogurt and lifts the dish's overall acidity", "fresh herbs add aromatic lift and freshness that complement the earthy sweetness of charred carrots"], "suggested_next": ["ingredient_change", "technique_step", "cost_recompute"]}
